questions.q: Who heads the Council of Ministers of the Union?
questions.explanation: The Council of Ministers is headed by the Prime Minister.

questions.q: The Council of Ministers aids and advises which constitutional authority in the exercise of executive functions?
questions.explanation: The Council of Ministers aids and advises the President in the exercise of executive functions.

questions.q: The Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to which House of Parliament?
questions.explanation: The Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: Who formally appoints the ministers of the Union Council of Ministers?
questions.explanation: Ministers are appointed by the President on the advice of the Prime Minister.

questions.q: The most senior tier within the Council of Ministers, often called the Union Cabinet, consists of which type of ministers?
questions.explanation: The Cabinet comprises the senior-most Cabinet Ministers who head important ministries.

questions.q: A minister who is not a member of either House of Parliament must become a member within how long?
questions.explanation: Any minister who is not a member of Parliament must secure membership within six months.

questions.q: Which of the following is one of the three categories of ministers in the Union Council of Ministers?
questions.explanation: The Council of Ministers includes Cabinet Ministers, Ministers of State, and Deputy Ministers.

questions.q: Collective responsibility means that if the Lok Sabha passes a no-confidence motion, what must the entire Council of Ministers do?
questions.explanation: If a no-confidence motion is passed, the whole Council of Ministers must resign.

questions.q: Who administers the oath of office and secrecy to a Union minister?
questions.explanation: The President administers the oath of office and secrecy to ministers.

questions.q: The Cabinet meetings are presided over by whom?
questions.explanation: Cabinet meetings are chaired by the Prime Minister, who leads the Council of Ministers.

questions.q: Which article of the Constitution provides for a Council of Ministers to aid and advise the President?
questions.explanation: Article 74 provides for a Council of Ministers, headed by the Prime Minister, to aid and advise the President.

questions.q: Which article states the principle of collective responsibility of the Council of Ministers to the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: Article 75 provides that the Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: The 91st Amendment of 2003 limits the size of the Council of Ministers to a maximum of what proportion of the total strength of the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: The 91st Amendment caps the Council of Ministers at fifteen per cent of the total strength of the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: A Minister of State holding 'independent charge' differs from an ordinary Minister of State in that the former does what?
options: Heads a ministry or department directly
Sits only in the Rajya Sabha
questions.explanation: A Minister of State with independent charge heads a ministry or department directly rather than under a Cabinet Minister.

questions.q: Under Article 77, the business of the Government of India is conducted in the name of whom?
questions.explanation: Article 77 provides that all executive action of the Government of India is taken in the name of the President.

questions.q: The salaries and allowances of ministers are determined by whom?
questions.explanation: The salaries and allowances of ministers are determined by Parliament by law.

questions.q: Who served as independent India's first Home Minister and Deputy Prime Minister within the early Council of Ministers?
questions.explanation: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel was independent India's first Deputy Prime Minister and Home Minister.

questions.q: The 91st Amendment also disqualifies a member disqualified under the anti-defection law from being appointed as what?
questions.explanation: The 91st Amendment bars a member disqualified on grounds of defection from being appointed a minister during that term.

questions.q: Which minister served as independent India's first Minister of Education within the Council of Ministers?
questions.explanation: Maulana Abul Kalam Azad served as independent India's first Minister of Education from 1947.

questions.q: The principle that ministers swim and sink together, supporting Cabinet decisions publicly, is known as what?
questions.explanation: Collective responsibility binds all ministers to support Cabinet decisions or resign.

questions.q: Who was the longest-serving member of the Union Cabinet, holding several portfolios over decades?
questions.explanation: Jagjivan Ram was the longest-serving member of the Indian Cabinet, holding several portfolios over decades.

questions.q: Which constitutional functionary is the administrative head of the Cabinet Secretariat assisting the Council of Ministers?
questions.explanation: The Cabinet Secretary is the senior-most civil servant and administrative head of the Cabinet Secretariat.

questions.q: Article 74(2) provides that the advice tendered by ministers to the President shall not be inquired into by which body?
questions.explanation: Article 74(2) bars courts from inquiring into the advice tendered by ministers to the President.

questions.q: The 91st Amendment, which capped the Council of Ministers and tightened anti-defection rules, was enacted in which year?
questions.explanation: The 91st Constitutional Amendment was enacted in 2003.

questions.q: If the President returns advice for reconsideration under Article 74(1), and the Council of Ministers reiterates it, the President must do what?
options: Act in accordance with the reiterated advice
Refer it to the Supreme Court
questions.explanation: The President may seek reconsideration once but must act in accordance with the advice if it is reiterated.

questions.q: The minimum size of the Council of Ministers in a state was not originally fixed; the 91st Amendment set a floor of how many ministers for states?
questions.explanation: The 91st Amendment fixed a minimum of twelve ministers for a state Council of Ministers.

questions.q: Under the Constitution, ministers hold office during the pleasure of which authority?
questions.explanation: Ministers hold office during the pleasure of the President, though in practice this follows the Prime Minister's advice.

questions.q: The word 'Cabinet' was inserted into the text of the Constitution (in Article 352) by which amendment?
questions.explanation: The 44th Amendment of 1978 inserted a reference to the Cabinet into Article 352 of the Constitution.

questions.q: A Deputy Minister within the Council of Ministers typically performs which role?
options: Assists a Cabinet Minister or Minister of State
questions.explanation: A Deputy Minister is the most junior rank and assists a Cabinet Minister or Minister of State, without independent charge.

questions.q: Which provision empowers the Prime Minister to advise the President to reallocate portfolios among ministers?
options: The Government of India Allocation of Business Rules under Article 77
questions.explanation: Allocation and transfer of business among ministers is governed by rules made under Article 77, on the Prime Minister's advice.
